data.title: Mexan’s Controlling Shareholder Moves to Place Entire Stake in Major Ownership Shake-Up
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Mexan ( (HK:0022) ).

Mexan Limited’s controlling shareholder, Winland Wealth (BVI) Limited, which is indirectly wholly owned by chairman and executive director Lun Yiu Kay Edwin, has entered into a placing agreement with Advent Securities (Hong Kong) Limited. The agreement covers the potential placing of between about 928.7 million and 1.33 billion existing shares at HK$0.17 each to independent investors.

The planned transaction represents up to approximately 67.47% of Mexan’s issued share capital and all of the shares currently held by the controlling shareholder, meaning that, if fully executed, Winland Wealth will cease to hold any shares and will no longer be a controlling shareholder. The placing agent has committed to ensure that no individual placee, alone or with concert parties, will hold 30% or more of Mexan’s issued share capital after completion, signaling an intention to diversify the shareholding base and avoid triggering mandatory takeover obligations.

Completion of the placing is scheduled on or before 14 April 2026, subject to the terms of the agreement, and the company has cautioned that the placing may or may not proceed. The potential exit of the controlling shareholder, and consequent shift to a more dispersed ownership structure, could materially reshape Mexan’s governance dynamics and market positioning, with investors advised to exercise caution when dealing in the shares during this transition period.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0022) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.28 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mexan stock, see the HK:0022 Stock Forecast page.

More about Mexan

Mexan Limited, listed in Hong Kong under stock code 22, is a Hong Kong-based company whose shares are held by a controlling shareholder through Winland Wealth (BVI) Limited. The board is led by chairman and executive director Lun Yiu Kay Edwin, with additional executive and independent non-executive directors overseeing corporate governance and compliance with local listing regulations.

The company operates under the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and the Securities and Futures Ordinance, indicating its focus on maintaining listing status and adherence to market disclosure rules. Its shareholder base and control structure are central to its corporate profile, with significant ownership previously concentrated in the hands of the controlling shareholder via the BVI entity.

Mexan’s governance structure features a mix of executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard corporate practices in Hong Kong’s listed market. This framework positions the company within the broader Hong Kong capital market, where changes in controlling ownership can materially affect investor perception and trading dynamics.

Average Trading Volume: 7,655,369

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$487.7M

For a thorough assessment of 0022 stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from JD.com, Inc. Class A ( (HK:9618) ) is now available.

JD.com, Inc. has priced an offering of CNY10 billion in CNY-denominated senior unsecured notes to be sold to certain non-U.S. investors in offshore transactions under Regulation S. The issuance comprises CNY7.5 billion of 2.05% notes due 2031 and CNY2.5 billion of 2.75% notes due 2036, with closing expected around April 10, 2026, subject to customary conditions.

The company plans to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including repaying existing debt and paying interest, in a move that should help optimize its capital structure and liquidity. The notes, which are not registered under the U.S. Securities Act, are expected to be listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, further integrating JD.com’s funding activities with Hong Kong’s debt capital market and offering investors additional exposure to its credit.

data.title: Melco International Strengthens Governance With Formal Audit Committee Mandate
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Melco International ( (HK:0200) ).

Melco International has formalised detailed terms of reference for its Audit Committee, defining its composition, responsibilities and powers in line with Hong Kong Stock Exchange governance requirements. The committee must comprise at least three non‑executive directors, mostly independent, with at least one member holding professional accounting or financial management expertise, and its chair and membership are appointed and removed by the board.

The Audit Committee is empowered to investigate company activities, access all books and records, obtain independent professional advice and report suspected fraud, internal control failures or legal breaches to the board. Its duties include overseeing the appointment and independence of external auditors, setting policies on non‑audit services, meeting auditors without executives present, and monitoring the integrity of the company’s financial reporting, reinforcing oversight and investor confidence in Melco’s governance framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0200) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Melco International stock, see the HK:0200 Stock Forecast page.

More about Melco International

Melco International Development Limited is a Hong Kong‑incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. It operates as an investment holding and development group with interests spanning leisure, entertainment and related services across Asian markets, and is subject to local corporate governance and listing regulations.

Bison Finance Group Limited ( (HK:0888) ) has shared an announcement.

BlockFin Holdings Limited has announced a change in the joint and several receivers overseeing 680,508,005 charged ordinary shares, representing a 30% controlling stake in the company. The receivership has shifted from two partners at PricewaterhouseCoopers to new appointees at Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu and Deloitte Advisory (Hong Kong), effective 31 March 2026.

The company said it has not received further details from the new receivers regarding plans for the controlling stake but indicated it will update the market if the receivers begin actively seeking or negotiating with potential buyers. The move underscores ongoing uncertainty around the ownership of BlockFin’s controlling block, with potential implications for future changes in control and any related takeover activity under Hong Kong’s Listing Rules and Takeovers Code.

An update from Seres Group Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9927) ) is now available.

Seres Group Co., Ltd. has issued a supplemental notice for its 2025 annual general meeting, revising an earlier agenda circulated on March 30, 2026. The change reflects the company’s governance practices as a Hong Kong-listed PRC joint stock company, where detailed shareholder approval is required for key capital management actions.

The company has replaced its original single special resolution on a proposal for share repurchase through centralized price bidding with eight separate special resolutions. This restructuring breaks out the purpose, type, method, implementation period, scale, pricing, funding source, and authorizations for the planned buyback, giving shareholders more granular items to consider and potentially enhancing transparency and control over the share repurchase program.

DTXS Silk Road Investment Holdings announced the resignation of independent non-executive director Dai Zhijie, who is stepping down from the board, audit committee and remuneration committee from 1 April 2026 to focus on other business commitments. data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Meitu ( (HK:1357) ) has provided an announcement.

Meitu has granted 16,456,530 share awards, equivalent to about 0.36% of its issued share capital excluding treasury shares, to employees and core service providers under its share award scheme, with vesting starting in April 2027. The awards, deliverable through a mix of new shares and treasury shares at the Board’s discretion, are intended to reward contributors to 2025 adjusted net profit growth, align interests with shareholders, conserve cash while expanding overseas via external consultants, and remain within Hong Kong listing rule limits, leaving 392,509,341 shares available for future grants.

The grant includes a Special Grant of 1,357 share awards each to most employees and service providers, with vesting over up to 24 months and no performance targets attached. By using equity incentives rather than cash to attract and retain talent, particularly in overseas markets where it hires through service providers, Meitu aims to support its international expansion and long-term development while maintaining compliance with individual and service provider caps under the share award scheme mandate.

Greentech Technology International Limited ( (HK:0195) ) just unveiled an update.

Greentech Technology International has issued a further update on its prolonged compliance challenges, which have led to delays in publishing its 2024 interim and annual results and its 2025 interim results, as well as a continuing suspension of trading in its shares. The company has been working under resumption guidance and has already conducted an independent forensic investigation and internal control review as part of efforts to address regulatory concerns.

In the latest development, Greentech disclosed details of an anonymous complaint lodged with its auditor, prompting the appointment of OCF as an independent investigator and now the engagement of an additional independent investigator. The allegations center on allegedly questionable fund flows at a former subsidiary between 2020 and 2021 and a purported payment to a former director from proceeds of a loan repayment, deepening scrutiny of the group’s past transactions and governance and potentially impacting investor confidence and the timetable for trading resumption.

The latest update is out from Million Cities Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2892) ).

Million Cities Holdings Limited has established formal terms of reference for its Executive Committee, clarifying its authority, responsibilities, and specific duties within the company’s governance structure. The move codifies that all executive directors and designated senior management will serve as members, with the committee chaired by an appointee of the board.

The new framework also specifies secretarial arrangements and meeting procedures, including who may convene meetings, advance notice requirements, and allowance for in-person, telephone, or video conference formats. data.title: Weigang Environmental Technology Wins HKEX Waiver to Appoint New Joint Company Secretary
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Weigang Environmental Technology Holding Group Ltd. ( (HK:1845) ) has issued an update.

Weigang Environmental Technology Holding Group Ltd. has appointed Xu Anli as joint company secretary effective 1 April 2026, highlighting her decade of experience in corporate investment, governance, compliance, and IPO matters, as well as her tenure as the company’s assistant secretary since December 2022. The board argues that her deep familiarity with the group’s operations and disclosure practices will strengthen secretarial functions and support corporate governance, capital management, and investor relations.

Because Xu does not yet meet the formal company secretary qualifications under Hong Kong Listing Rule 3.28, Weigang obtained a three-year conditional waiver from the Stock Exchange, requiring that she be assisted by qualified joint secretary Ms. Pang and complete at least 15 hours of relevant training annually. The waiver, which can be revoked in case of material Listing Rules breaches, underscores both the exchange’s flexibility and the company’s dependence on Xu’s expertise to maintain regulatory compliance while its main business remains outside Hong Kong.
